---
description: "Guidance for creating more accessible code"
applyTo: "**/*.html, **/*.css, **/*.js"
---

# Accessibility Instructions

You are an expert in accessibility with deep software engineering expertise.

## Non-negotiables (MUST)

- Conform to [WCAG 2.2 Level AA](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/).
- Go beyond minimum conformance when it meaningfully improves usability.
- If the project uses a UI component library, use component patterns as defined by the library.
- Prefer native HTML elements/attributes over ARIA.
- Use ARIA only when necessary (do not add ARIA to native elements when native semantics already work).
- Ensure correct accessible **name, role, value, states, and properties**.
- All interactive elements are keyboard operable, with clearly visible focus, and no keyboard traps.
- Do not claim the output is "fully accessible".

## Inclusive Language (MUST)

- Use respectful, inclusive, people-first language in any user-facing text.
- Avoid stereotypes or assumptions about ability, cognition, or experience.

## Cognitive Load (SHOULD)

- Prefer plain language.
- Use consistent page structure (landmarks).
- Keep navigation order consistent.
- Keep the interface clean and simple.

## Structure and Semantics

### Page Structure (MUST)

- Use landmarks (`header`, `nav`, `main`, `footer`) appropriately.
- Use headings to introduce new sections of content; avoid skipping heading levels.
- Prefer one `h1` for the page topic.

### Page Title (SHOULD)

- Set a descriptive `<title>`. Prefer: "Unique page - section - site".

## Keyboard and Focus

### Core Rules (MUST)

- All interactive elements are keyboard operable.
- Tab order follows reading order and is predictable.
- Focus is always visible.
- Hidden content is not focusable (`hidden`, `display:none`, `visibility:hidden`).
- Static content MUST NOT be tabbable. Exception: `tabindex="-1"` for programmatic focus.

### Skip Link (MUST)

Provide a skip link as the first focusable element:

```html
<header>
  <a href="#maincontent" class="sr-only">Skip to main content</a>
</header>
<main id="maincontent" tabindex="-1">
  <h1><!-- page title --></h1>
</main>
```

```css
.sr-only:not(:focus):not(:active) {
  clip: rect(0 0 0 0);
  clip-path: inset(50%);
  height: 1px;
  overflow: hidden;
  position: absolute;
  white-space: nowrap;
  width: 1px;
}
```

## Low Vision and Contrast (MUST)

### Contrast Requirements (MUST)

- Text contrast: at least 4.5:1 (large text: 3:1).
- Focus indicators and key control boundaries: at least 3:1 vs adjacent colors.
- Do not rely on color alone to convey information. Provide text and/or icons.

### Color Generation Rules (MUST)

- Do not invent arbitrary colors. Use project-approved design tokens (CSS variables).
- Avoid alpha for text and key UI affordances — contrast becomes background-dependent.
- Ensure contrast for all interactive states: default, hover, active, focus, visited, disabled.

### RenWeb Dark Theme Contrast Reference

| Element | Color | Background | Ratio | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Body text | `#e0e0e0` | `#242424` | ≈ 7.6:1 | ✓ PASS |
| Secondary text | `#b0b0b0` | `#242424` | ≈ 5.5:1 | ✓ PASS |
| Muted text | `#888888` | `#1a1a1a` | ≈ 3.7:1 | ⚠ hints only |
| Status success | `#10b981` | `#242424` | ≈ 4.6:1 | ✓ PASS |
| Status error | `#ef4444` | `#242424` | ≈ 4.7:1 | ✓ PASS |
| Cyan heading | `#00ffff` | `#1a1a1a` | ≈ 14:1 | ✓ PASS |

## High Contrast / Forced Colors Mode (MUST)

- Never override or disrupt OS accessibility settings.
- The UI MUST adapt to High Contrast / Forced Colors mode automatically.

```css
@media (forced-colors: active) {
  .button {
    border: 2px solid ButtonBorder;
  }
}

/* if using box-shadow for focus, also use a transparent outline */
.button:focus {
  box-shadow: 0 0 4px 3px rgba(90, 50, 200, .7);
  outline: 2px solid transparent;
}
```

- Use system color keywords (`ButtonText`, `ButtonBorder`, `CanvasText`, `Canvas`).
- Do not use `forced-color-adjust: none` unless absolutely necessary.

### Icons (MUST)

- Icons MUST adapt to text color. Prefer `currentColor` for SVG icon fills/strokes:

```css
svg {
  fill: currentColor;
  stroke: currentColor;
}
```

## Reflow (WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.10) (MUST)

- Multi-line text must fit within 320px wide containers without two-dimensional scrolling.
- Use responsive layout primitives (`flex`, `grid`) with fluid sizing; enable text wrapping.
- Avoid fixed widths that force two-dimensional scrolling at 320px.
- In flex/grid layouts, ensure children can shrink/wrap (`min-width: 0`).
- Handle long strings with `overflow-wrap: anywhere`.

## Controls and Labels

- Every interactive element has a visible label that persists while entering text.
- The accessible name of each interactive element MUST contain the visible label text.
- If multiple controls share the same visible label, use `aria-label` adding context.

## Forms

- Every form control has a programmatic label. Prefer `<label for="...">`.
- Indicate required fields visually (`*`) and programmatically (`aria-required="true"`).
- Use `aria-invalid="true"` for invalid fields; associate errors with `aria-describedby`.
- On submit with invalid input, focus the first invalid control.

## Graphics and Images

- Informative images: use `alt` on `<img>`, `role="img"` + `aria-label` on `<svg>`.
- Decorative images: `alt=""` on `<img>`, `aria-hidden="true"` on other elements.

## Tables

- Use `<table>` for static tabular data with `<th>` to associate headers.
- Column headers in the first row; row headers use `<th>` in each row.

## Final Verification Checklist (MUST)

Before finalizing output, explicitly verify:

- Structure: landmarks, headings, one `h1` for the page topic.
- Keyboard and focus: operable controls, visible focus, predictable tab order, no traps.
- Controls and labels: visible labels present and included in accessible names.
- Forms: labels, required indicators, errors (`aria-invalid` + `aria-describedby`).
- Contrast: meets 4.5:1 / 3:1 thresholds, color not the only cue.
- Forced colors: does not break OS High Contrast / Forced Colors mode.
